seo.title: Building Workflow Automation with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build workflow automation with OpenAI Assistants API + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building workflow automation with OpenAI Assistants API enables powerful multi-step agents that handle API integrations, conditional logic, and human approvals using built-in tools like code interpreter and file search. However, risks like hallucinated function calls, unsafe code execution, and context bloat can lead to failed workflows, security breaches, or incorrect actions. Vex provides essential guardrails through verification webhooks to ensure action correctness, permissions, idempotency, and rollback safety.

architectureOverview.heading: Workflow Automation Architecture with OpenAI Assistants API
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture centers on OpenAI Assistant threads that maintain conversation state across multiple runs. Each run processes user instructions using tools like function calling for API integrations, code interpreter for data processing, and file search for document retrieval. Conditional logic is implemented via structured instructions and tool outputs, with human-in-the-loop via status polling and approval steps.

External systems integrate via webhooks on run completion events, enabling verification before final outputs. Vex sits as a verification layer, checking tool outputs against schemas, validating permissions, ensuring idempotency, and enabling rollbacks for failed verifications. This creates a resilient pipeline for production workflows.

Key components include thread management for state, run orchestration for execution, tool definitions for integrations, and event webhooks for observability and safety.

risks.name: Code interpreter executing unsafe operations in sandbox
risks.description: Agent may generate and execute malicious code like file deletions or network calls despite sandboxing
risks.mitigation: Vex scans code execution outputs and blocks unsafe operations before completion

risks.name: File search retrieving irrelevant or outdated documents
risks.description: Retriever returns wrong context leading to hallucinated decisions in workflow steps
risks.mitigation: Vex validates retrieved file relevance against query semantics and recency

risks.name: Thread context growing beyond model limits silently
risks.description: Long-running threads exceed token limits causing degraded performance or failures
risks.mitigation: Vex monitors thread token usage and triggers context summarization or truncation

risks.name: Function call arguments hallucinated outside valid schema
risks.description: Agent generates invalid JSON parameters breaking downstream API integrations
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validates all tool calls before execution with auto-correction

implementation.title: Create Assistant with Workflow Tools
implementation.description: Define assistant with function tools for API integrations and workflow steps

implementation.title: Thread and Run Management
implementation.description: Create persistent thread and execute multi-step workflow runs
implementation.code:
thread = client.beta.threads.create()

run = client.beta.threads.runs.create(
    thread_id=thread.id,
    assistant_id=assistant.id,
    instructions="Execute invoice processing workflow: 1. Fetch data 2. Validate 3. Send approval"
)

# Poll for completion
while run.status in ['queued', 'in_progress']:
    run = client.beta.threads.runs.retrieve(thread_id=thread.id, run_id=run.id)
    time.sleep(1)

messages = client.beta.threads.messages.list(thread_id=thread.id)
